title: Sarcochilus philippinensis
intro: Sarcochilus philippinensis, also known as the Philippine orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Philippines. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Sarcochilus philippinensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sarcochilus philippinensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be supported by scientific evidence and its safety should be evaluated.
